task_completion.description: Open the target rack elevation diagram in Draw.io
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Draw.io application is open and displaying the 'rack_a07.drawio' diagram.
The label 'DB-REPLICA-01' is no longer visible anywhere in the rack diagram.

task_completion.description: Add new devices and relocate the cable management panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rack diagram contains new labels exactly matching: 'DB-REPLICA-02', 'SW-DIST-01', 'APP-API-01', 'APP-API-02', and 'Patch Panel'. The 'Cable Management Panel' must also be present in the rack.

Devices in the rack are colored according to their function: Database servers (e.g., DB-REPLICA-02) are pastel orange, Network devices (e.g., SW-DIST-01) are pastel blue, Compute servers (e.g., APP-API) are pastel green, and Power equipment is pastel red.

task_completion.description: Create a second page for the Power Budget table
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second page or tab titled 'Power Budget' is visible in Draw.io, containing a table with device names and their corresponding power consumption (wattage) data.

task_completion.description: Export the multi-page diagram to PDF
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Draw.io 'Export as PDF' dialog is used with multi-page settings, or a file manager/terminal shows 'rack_a07_audit.pdf' successfully created in the '~/Diagrams/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Draw.io graphical interface to edit the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the Draw.io GUI (moving shapes, changing colors, adding tabs) rather than editing the .drawio XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent exported the PDF using Draw.io
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses Draw.io's built-in export functionality (File -> Export as -> PDF) rather than using a command-line conversion tool or spoofing the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just print the expected text to the terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected device names and power budget data are actually placed inside the Draw.io canvas, not just echoed into a terminal or written to a plain text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram is located at ~/Diagrams/rack_a07.drawio. The target PDF export path is ~/Diagrams/exports/rack_a07_audit.pdf. The diagram must NOT contain 'DB-REPLICA-01'. It MUST contain 'DB-REPLICA-02', 'SW-DIST-01', 'APP-API-01', 'APP-API-02', and 'Patch Panel'. The required color codes are #f8cecc (Red/Power), #dae8fc (Blue/Network), #d5e8d4 (Green/Compute), and #ffe6cc (Orange/Database).
